informal: Suppose $\left(e_{1}, \ldots, e_{m}\right)$ is an orthonormal list of vectors in $V$. Let $v \in V$. Prove that $\|v\|^{2}=\left|\left\langle v, e_{1}\right\rangle\right|^{2}+\cdots+\left|\left\langle v, e_{m}\right\rangle\right|^{2}$ if and only if $v \in \operatorname{span}\left(e_{1}, \ldots, e_{m}\right)$.
reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal claim. The hypothesis `Orthonormal F e` for `e : Fin n → V` corresponds to "(e_1, ..., e_m) is an orthonormal list", v is an arbitrary vector, and the conclusion is the desired iff. On the LHS of the iff, `∑ i : Fin n, ‖⟪v, e i⟫_F‖^2` uses the norm on the scalar field F (with `RCLike F`), which equals |⟨v, e_i⟩|, so this is exactly Σ |⟨v, e_i⟩|². On the RHS, `Submodule.span F (e '' Set.univ)` is the span of the set {e i | i : Fin n}, i.e. span(e_1, ..., e_m). The setup over `RCLike F` correctly covers both real and complex inner product spaces as in Axler.
